title: Managing Customers with Next-Generation Software Applications: 1998 Edition (CIS Report)
author: Hugh Bishop, Chris Fletcher, Chris Martins, Chris Pavlic
abstract: Aberdeen Group's fourth annual Customer Interaction Software (CIS) report surveyed the rapidly consolidating market for front-line business applications including sales force automation, customer service, telemarketing/telesales, and emerging marketing automation. The 1998 edition introduced web-based CIS as the future architecture and documented convergence with enterprise business applications (EBAs), profiling over fifty CIS suppliers across eight application modules. It identified the network-centric CIS architecture and CIS-to-EBA integration as the two defining trends of the next phase of market development.
importance_rationale: Aberdeen's fourth annual CIS report captured the market at its peak consolidation moment — within 12 months of publication, Clarify was acquired by Nortel for $2.1B, Vantive by PeopleSoft for $433M, and Siebel was racing to dominate the category. The report's comprehensive supplier matrix became a reference for enterprise buyers navigating a rapidly consolidating field.
relevance_rationale: CRM/CIS as a market category remains central to enterprise software; the analytical frameworks for evaluating SFA, customer service, and marketing automation modules transfer directly to modern CRM evaluation. Siebel's trajectory (dominant in 1998, acquired by Oracle in 2006 for $5.85B) validates the consolidation path Aberdeen documented.
prescience_rationale: Aberdeen predicted web-architected CIS would represent the future of the market — this proved correct as Salesforce.com (founded 1999) validated the web-CRM thesis and CRM-ERP convergence occurred exactly as predicted via the acquisitions of Vantive (by PeopleSoft) and Siebel (by Oracle).
